name: Bench Dips
shortDescription: Bench dips are a bodyweight exercise targeting the triceps by lowering and raising the body using a bench for support.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a stable bench and place your hands next to your hips, fingers forward. Slide your hips off the edge, keeping your legs extended, and lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, then press back up to the starting position.
steps: Sit on a bench and place your hands beside your hips, gripping the edge.
Extend your legs out in front with heels on the floor.
Slide your hips off the bench, supporting your weight with your arms.
Lower your body by bending your elbows to about 90 degrees.
Push through your palms to extend your elbows and return to the start.
breathing: Inhale as you lower down, exhale as you press upward.
